info.title: OpenMail API
info.description: Email infrastructure API for AI agents. Create inboxes, send and receive email, and get real-time notifications via webhooks.

clientId.description: Your own identifier for the pod, if set.

isDefault.description: Whether this is the account's default pod. Every account has exactly one; it is created automatically and cannot be deleted.

name.description: Human-readable label for the key, if set.

podId.description: ID of the pod this key is scoped to, or null for an account-wide key. A pod-scoped key can only access resources in this pod.

inboxId.description: ID of the inbox this key is confined to, or null. When set, the key can only access this single inbox and its threads, messages, and drafts (its `podId` is the inbox's pod).

tokenPrefix.description: Non-secret leading characters of the token, to help you identify it.

last4.description: Last 4 characters of the token, shown alongside the prefix.

lastUsedAt.description: When the key last authenticated a request, or null if never used.

ApiKeyWithToken.description: An API key plus its raw `token`. The token is returned only once, at creation, and is never retrievable again.

token.description: The secret API key. Store it securely — it is shown only once and cannot be retrieved later.

podId.description: ID of the pod this inbox belongs to. Inboxes created without an explicit pod land in your account's default pod.

webhookUrl.description: URL that receives signed webhook events for this inbox, if set. Overrides the account-level webhook for this inbox only.

webhookSecret.description: Signing secret for this inbox's webhook. Only present in the response when the secret is generated or rotated (on create, on first setting a webhook URL, or via the rotate-secret endpoint) — it is never returned afterward.

toAddr.description: Your OpenMail inbox address for inbound messages. For outbound sends, the primary recipient.

headerTo.description: Inbound only. The address the sender wrote to (comma-separated if multiple). When deliveryRole is cc, reply to this address — not fromAddr.

deliveryRole.description: Inbound only. to if your inbox was the main recipient; cc if you were copied on the email.

cc.description: Email addresses copied on the message.

isRead.description: Whether the thread has been marked as read. Defaults to false for new inbound threads. Auto-set to true when a reply is sent.

post.description: Create a new pod (an isolated sub-account for one of your end users or tenants). Pass your own `clientId` to map the pod to a record in your system and address it later without storing the OpenMail ID.

clientId.description: Your own stable identifier for this pod (e.g. your user/tenant ID). Must be unique within your account. Use it in place of the pod ID on any pod or inbox endpoint.

name.description: Human-readable label shown in the console.

409.description: A pod with the same `clientId` already exists in your account

schema.description: A pod with the same `clientId` already exists in your account

get.description: List all pods for your account. The default pod is always returned first. Supports pagination.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired API key

schema.description: Missing, invalid, or expired API key

get.description: Retrieve a single pod by its OpenMail ID or your own `clientId`.

patch.description: Update a pod's `clientId` or `name`. Only the fields you include are changed.

clientId.description: Your own identifier for this pod. Set null to clear.

name.description: Human-readable label. Set null to clear.

403.description: The authenticating key is pod-scoped and cannot modify pods

schema.description: The authenticating key is pod-scoped and cannot modify pods

409.description: A pod with the same `clientId` already exists in your account

schema.description: A pod with the same `clientId` already exists in your account

delete.description: Permanently delete a pod. The pod must be empty (no inboxes or pod-scoped domains) and cannot be the default pod.

409.description: Pod still has inboxes (`pod_not_empty`), still owns pod-scoped domains (`pod_has_domains`), or is the default pod (`default_pod`)

schema.description: Pod still has inboxes (`pod_not_empty`), still owns pod-scoped domains (`pod_has_domains`), or is the default pod (`default_pod`)

post.description: Mint an API key scoped to this pod. The key can read and send from the pod's inboxes and create new inboxes in this pod; it can never delete an inbox, change an inbox's webhook config, manage pods, mint keys, or reach another pod's data. The full `token` is returned once, here — store it securely, it cannot be retrieved again. Requires an account-wide key (a pod-scoped key cannot mint keys).

name.description: Human-readable label to identify the key later.

403.description: The authenticating key is pod-scoped and cannot mint keys

schema.description: The authenticating key is pod-scoped and cannot mint keys

422.description: The pod already has the maximum number of active keys (`api_key_limit_reached`)

schema.description: The pod already has the maximum number of active keys (`api_key_limit_reached`)

get.description: List the (non-revoked) API keys scoped to this pod. Only non-secret fields are returned — the token itself is never retrievable after creation.

403.description: The authenticating key is pod-scoped and cannot manage keys

schema.description: The authenticating key is pod-scoped and cannot manage keys

delete.description: Permanently revoke a pod-scoped API key. Any integration using it immediately starts getting 401s. Requires an account-wide key.

parameters.name: keyId
parameters.description: The API key ID to revoke

403.description: The authenticating key is pod-scoped and cannot manage keys

schema.description: The authenticating key is pod-scoped and cannot manage keys

post.description: Create a new email inbox. Optionally set a sender display name.

displayName.description: Sender display name shown in the From header

domain.description: Domain for the inbox address. Defaults to your account domain (e.g. `openmail.sh`). Pass a verified custom domain you own to create the inbox on it (e.g. `agent-mail.example.com`). The domain must already be added and verified for your account; the account default never switches automatically.

webhookUrl.description: URL to receive signed webhook events for this inbox (e.g. inbound mail). Overrides the account-level webhook for this inbox only. A signing secret is generated and returned once in the response body.

podId.description: Pod to create the inbox in. Accepts the pod ID or your own `clientId`. Omit to place the inbox in your account's default pod.

400.description: Invalid mailbox name, `domain` is not verified or not available in the selected pod, or `podId` does not match a pod in your account

schema.description: Invalid mailbox name, `domain` is not verified or not available in the selected pod, or `podId` does not match a pod in your account

402.description: Pro plan: inbox creation is paused because the account's monthly spend limit was reached

schema.description: Pro plan: inbox creation is paused because the account's monthly spend limit was reached

422.description: Plan inbox limit reached, or `webhookUrl` failed validation (`invalid_webhook_url`)

schema.description: Plan inbox limit reached, or `webhookUrl` failed validation (`invalid_webhook_url`)

get.description: List all inboxes for your account. Supports pagination.

parameters.name: podId
parameters.description: Filter to inboxes in a single pod. Accepts the pod ID or your own `clientId`. An unknown value returns an empty list.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired API key

schema.description: Missing, invalid, or expired API key

get.description: Retrieve a single inbox by its ID, including its address, display name, and creation time.

patch.description: Update an inbox's settings, such as the sender display name shown in the From header.

displayName.description: Sender display name. Set null to clear.

webhookUrl.description: URL to receive signed webhook events for this inbox. Set null to clear and revert to the account-level webhook. A signing secret is generated the first time a webhook URL is set on this inbox and returned once in the response body.

403.description: The authenticating key is inbox-scoped (read + send only) and cannot modify the inbox

schema.description: The authenticating key is inbox-scoped (read + send only) and cannot modify the inbox

delete.description: Permanently delete an inbox and stop delivery to its address. This action cannot be undone.

403.description: The authenticating key is inbox-scoped (read + send only) and cannot delete the inbox

schema.description: The authenticating key is inbox-scoped (read + send only) and cannot delete the inbox

post.description: Generate a new signing secret for this inbox's webhook. The inbox must already have a webhook URL set. The previous secret is invalidated immediately.

400.description: No webhook URL is set on this inbox (`no_webhook_url`)

schema.description: No webhook URL is set on this inbox (`no_webhook_url`)

403.description: The authenticating key is inbox-scoped (read + send only) and cannot rotate webhook secrets

schema.description: The authenticating key is inbox-scoped (read + send only) and cannot rotate webhook secrets

post.description: Send a signed test event to this inbox's webhook URL. Requires a webhook URL (and signing secret) to already be set on the inbox.

400.description: No webhook URL is set on this inbox (`no_webhook_url`)

schema.description: No webhook URL is set on this inbox (`no_webhook_url`)

502.description: The webhook endpoint returned an error status, or the request to it failed (`webhook_failed`)

schema.description: The webhook endpoint returned an error status, or the request to it failed (`webhook_failed`)

post.description: Mint an API key scoped to this inbox. The key can only read and send from this one inbox — its threads, messages, and drafts — and can never reach another inbox, manage pods, or mint keys. The full `token` is returned once, here — store it securely, it cannot be retrieved again. Requires an account-wide key (a scoped key cannot mint keys).

name.description: Human-readable label to identify the key later.

403.description: The authenticating key is scoped and cannot mint keys

schema.description: The authenticating key is scoped and cannot mint keys

409.description: The inbox is not assigned to a pod, so an inbox-scoped key cannot be minted for it (`inbox_has_no_pod`)

schema.description: The inbox is not assigned to a pod, so an inbox-scoped key cannot be minted for it (`inbox_has_no_pod`)

422.description: The inbox already has the maximum number of active keys (`api_key_limit_reached`)

schema.description: The inbox already has the maximum number of active keys (`api_key_limit_reached`)

get.description: List the (non-revoked) API keys scoped to this inbox. Only non-secret fields are returned — the token itself is never retrievable after creation.

403.description: The authenticating key is scoped and cannot manage keys

schema.description: The authenticating key is scoped and cannot manage keys

delete.description: Permanently revoke an inbox-scoped API key. Any integration using it immediately starts getting 401s. Requires an account-wide key.

parameters.name: keyId
parameters.description: The API key ID to revoke

403.description: The authenticating key is scoped and cannot manage keys

schema.description: The authenticating key is scoped and cannot manage keys

post.description: Send an email from an inbox. Requires Idempotency-Key header. Use threadId to reply to an existing thread. When replying on a thread where your inbox was CC'd (deliveryRole cc), the original sender is automatically CC'd.

subject.description: Required for new threads. Optional when threadId is set — the thread subject is used automatically (with Re: prefix).

body.description: Email body. Plain text or HTML — HTML is auto-detected and rendered, with a plain-text fallback generated automatically.

bodyHtml.description: Provide only when the plain-text and HTML versions must differ. Omit for normal sends — put HTML in `body` instead.

includeQuote.description: When replying with threadId, append a quoted copy of the previous message to the body. Defaults to true. Set false to send only your reply text.

replyTo.description: Optional. Address that replies should be routed to (set as the `Reply-To` MIME header). Free plan: must be the address of an inbox you own in OpenMail. Pro plan or higher: any valid email address.

subject.description: Required for new threads. Optional when threadId is set — the thread subject is used automatically (with Re: prefix).

body.description: Email body. Plain text or HTML — HTML is auto-detected and rendered, with a plain-text fallback generated automatically.

bodyHtml.description: Provide only when the plain-text and HTML versions must differ. Omit for normal sends — put HTML in `body` instead.

includeQuote.description: When replying with threadId, append a quoted copy of the previous message to the body. Defaults to true. Set false to send only your reply text.

replyTo.description: Optional. Address that replies should be routed to (set as the `Reply-To` MIME header). Free plan: must be the address of an inbox you own in OpenMail. Pro plan or higher: any valid email address.

attachments.description: File attachments (max 25 MB total per email)

parameters.name: idempotency-key
parameters.description: Unique key for idempotent sends (e.g. UUID)

400.description: Missing Idempotency-Key, missing request body, invalid 'to'/'replyTo'/'cc' address, or missing subject when required

schema.description: Missing Idempotency-Key, missing request body, invalid 'to'/'replyTo'/'cc' address, or missing subject when required

402.description: Sending is paused because this account's monthly spend limit was reached (`spend_cap_paused`)

schema.description: Sending is paused because this account's monthly spend limit was reached (`spend_cap_paused`)

403.description: Feature requires a paid plan. Returned when a free-plan customer sets `replyTo` to an address that is not one of their own OpenMail inboxes (`feature_requires_paid_plan`), the account is banned (`account_banned`), or sending is suspended due to deliverability/abuse signals (`reputation_suspended`).

schema.description: Feature requires a paid plan. Returned when a free-plan customer sets `replyTo` to an address that is not one of their own OpenMail inboxes (`feature_requires_paid_plan`), the account is banned (`account_banned`), or sending is suspended due to deliverability/abuse signals (`reputation_suspended`).

422.description: Content blocked (`unsafe_content`, `phishing_detected`), recipient domain has no MX records (`no_mx_records`), recipient suppressed for bounce/complaint (`recipient_suppressed`), or account storage limit exceeded (`storage_limit_exceeded`)

schema.description: Content blocked (`unsafe_content`, `phishing_detected`), recipient domain has no MX records (`no_mx_records`), recipient suppressed for bounce/complaint (`recipient_suppressed`), or account storage limit exceeded (`storage_limit_exceeded`)

429.description: Rate limit exceeded (global daily, per-inbox daily, per-inbox burst) or cold outreach limit. Check the Retry-After response header.

schema.description: Rate limit exceeded (global daily, per-inbox daily, per-inbox burst) or cold outreach limit. Check the Retry-After response header.

get.description: Returns a 302 redirect to a signed URL for the attachment.

Extracts and returns the plain text content of an attachment. Supports PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, images (via OCR), and other common formats. The result is cached on the message after the first extraction.

extractionMethod.description: Method used to extract text (e.g. pdf, docx, ocr)

422.description: Text could not be extracted from the attachment

schema.description: Text could not be extracted from the attachment

get.description: List all messages in an inbox, across every thread. Supports pagination for high-volume inboxes.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired API key

schema.description: Missing, invalid, or expired API key

get.description: List threads for an inbox. Use is_read to filter by read status — for example, ?is_read=false returns only unread threads.

parameters.name: is_read
parameters.description: Filter by read status. true = read threads only, false = unread threads only. Omit to return all.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired API key

schema.description: Missing, invalid, or expired API key

get.description: Retrieve a thread and all of its messages in order, with read status and full message content.

isRead.description: Whether the thread has been marked as read

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired API key

schema.description: Missing, invalid, or expired API key

patch.description: Update a thread's read status. Use this to mark a thread as read after your agent has processed it, or as unread to re-queue it for later processing.

is_read.description: Set to true to mark as read, false to mark as unread

tags.name: pods
tags.description: Isolated sub-accounts for your end users or tenants

tags.name: inboxes
tags.description: Email inboxes and their webhook configuration

tags.name: attachments
tags.description: Downloading and extracting text from attachments
